title: Chain Reaction
learning_objectives: Chain multiple exploits into coordinated attack
Maintain persistence through privilege boundary crossing
scenario: Scenario A web application has no single critical vulnerability — but SSRF, misconfigured internal API, and exposed debug endpoint may chain to RCE. Subtasks Scoring Rubric Linked Recipes: RA05, RA06 ________________________________________________________________________________ 3.3 Evasion & Anti-Detection

scenario_context.setting: Lagos, Nigeria, West African tech startup hub
scenario_context.challenge: Chain exploits to escalate privileges and establish foothold in West African tech startup hub's Lagos, Nigeria network
scenario_context.relevance: Reflects real-world African enterprise security challenges and red team operations across the continent

subtasks.title: Vulnerability Mapping
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Begin by thoroughly reviewing the scenario and understanding all objectives. Document your approach before execution. This is the foundational subtask that enables all following work.

Specific to this mission:
- Follow all engagement rules of engagement
- Maintain OPSEC throughout execution
- Collect evidence of all activities
- Document findings for client reporting
- Coordinate with engagement lead and blue team as needed

subtasks.title: Chain Identification
subtasks.description: Identify 2+ vulnerability chains with step-by-step mapping.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Conduct systematic reconnaissance and information gathering. Map the target network, identify services, and document all findings with evidence.

Specific to this mission:
- Follow all engagement rules of engagement
- Maintain OPSEC throughout execution
- Collect evidence of all activities
- Document findings for client reporting
- Coordinate with engagement lead and blue team as needed

subtasks.title: Chain Exploitation
subtasks.description: Exploit the most promising chain to achieve RCE.
subtasks.evidence: Chain exploitation log with per-step evidence
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Analyze vulnerabilities discovered in reconnaissance. Validate each vulnerability through testing and document the exploitation path.

Specific to this mission:
- Follow all engagement rules of engagement
- Maintain OPSEC throughout execution
- Collect evidence of all activities
- Document findings for client reporting
- Coordinate with engagement lead and blue team as needed
subtasks.evidence_required: Chain exploitation log with per-step evidence

subtasks.title: Automated PoC
subtasks.description: Python script automating the complete chain.
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Execute the exploitation with careful attention to OPSEC and evasion. Maintain detailed logs of all actions for evidence and reporting.

Specific to this mission:
- Follow all engagement rules of engagement
- Maintain OPSEC throughout execution
- Collect evidence of all activities
- Document findings for client reporting
- Coordinate with engagement lead and blue team as needed

subtasks.title: LOLBins Post-Exploitation
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Establish persistence mechanisms that survive system reboots and will maintain access throughout the engagement.

Specific to this mission:
- Follow all engagement rules of engagement
- Maintain OPSEC throughout execution
- Collect evidence of all activities
- Document findings for client reporting
- Coordinate with engagement lead and blue team as needed

subtasks.title: Technical Advisory
subtasks.detailed_guidance:
Perform privilege escalation and lateral movement to achieve the mission objectives. Document each pivot point.

Specific to this mission:
- Follow all engagement rules of engagement
- Maintain OPSEC throughout execution
- Collect evidence of all activities
- Document findings for client reporting
- Coordinate with engagement lead and blue team as needed

quiz.question: What is the primary advantage of chaining multiple exploits?
options: It makes the attack slower and easier to detect
It demonstrates full attack chain from initial access to objective achievement
It eliminates the need for privilege escalation
quiz.explanation: Exploit chains demonstrate complete attack paths from initial compromise to objective achievement.

quiz.question: In a privilege escalation attack, what is the goal of lateral movement?
options: To access different systems or accounts to progress toward mission objectives
quiz.explanation: Lateral movement provides access to additional systems and accounts necessary to achieve engagement objectives.

mentor_notes: For OA-M04 - Chain Reaction: Ensure learner demonstrates proper OPSEC, evidence collection, and professional reporting. This mission is designed to build advanced exploitation expertise. Coordinate with blue team for detection validation where applicable.
